qualifying nature) and Professional Knowledge (100 marks)
composite duration of 120 minutes. Personal Interview: 100 marks
conducted for candidates shortlisted based on merit. Final Merit List: Prepared based on combined scores of Online Examination and Interview.

content_sections.heading: Bank of India Credit Officer Recruitment 2026 PDF Download Link
content_sections.text: Candidates can download the official Bank of India Credit Officer Recruitment 2026 PDF to check complete eligibility, vacancy, and application details before applying. The notification PDF was released on 01 June 2026 along with Annexure-I detailing post-wise qualifications. Download Bank of India Credit Officer Recruitment 2026 PDF Direct Link to Apply Bank of India Credit Officer Recruitment 2026

content_sections.heading: Bank of India Credit Officer Bharti 2026 – Quick Overview
content_sections.text: Candidates searching for the BOI Credit Officer Vacancy 2026 details can refer to the quick summary below for an instant overview of the post, organization, vacancy count, last date, and application mode.

content_sections.heading: Bank of India Credit Officer Recruitment 2026 – Important Dates
content_sections.text: Candidates wondering about the Bank of India Credit Officer Recruitment 2026 timeline can note that the online application process starts on 30 June 2026 and closes on 20 July 2026. The relevant date for determining age and qualification eligibility is fixed as 01 June 2026.

content_sections.heading: Bank of India Credit Officer Vacancy 2026 – Post-wise Breakdown
content_sections.text: The BOI Credit Officer Vacancy 2026 is distributed across three grades: MMGS-II, MMGS-III, and SMGS-IV. Out of the total 779 vacancies, 475 are unfilled vacancies carried forward from the previous recruitment Project 2025-26/01.

content_sections.heading: Bank of India Credit Officer Recruitment 2026 Eligibility Criteria
content_sections.text: Candidates checking eligibility for the Bank of India Credit Officer Recruitment 2026 should note that qualification and experience requirements vary by grade. A graduation degree with 60% marks (55% for SC/ST/OBC/PWBD) or professional qualifications like CA/CMA-ICWA/CS/CFA is required, along with post-qualification banking/credit experience. Age relaxation is applicable as follows: 5 years for SC/ST , 3 years for OBC (Non-creamy layer) , 10 years for Persons with Benchmark Disabilities , and 5 years for Ex-Servicemen and persons affected by the 1984 riots. A Degree (Graduation) in any discipline from recognized University/Institute with 60% marks or equivalent grade (55% for SC/ ST/ OBC/ PWBD) recognized by the Govt. of India or any equivalent qualification recognized as such by the Central Government or CA /CMA-ICWA/CS/CFA

rows.minimum_experience: 3 years post-qualification experience as an officer, including 2 years in MSME/Commercial Credit/Project Finance
5 years post-qualification experience as an officer, including 3 years in MSME/Commercial Credit/Project Finance
7 years post-qualification experience as an officer, including 4 years in MSME/Commercial Credit/Project Finance

content_sections.heading: Bank of India Credit Officer Application Fee 2026
content_sections.text: Candidates applying for the BOI Credit Officer Recruitment Apply Online 2026 must pay the application fee online between 30 June and 20 July 2026. The fee differs by category as shown below. Payment can be made using Debit/Credit Cards, Internet Banking, UPI, or Mobile Wallets. Bank transaction charges, if any, will be borne by the candidate.

content_sections.heading: Bank of India Credit Officer Salary 2026 – Pay Scale
content_sections.text: Candidates wondering about the salary structure for the Bank of India Credit Officer Recruitment 2026 can refer to the pay scale below for each grade. Calculate your exact in-hand Salary in 10 seconds - Calculate Now

content_sections.heading: Bank of India Credit Officer Recruitment 2026 Selection Process
content_sections.text: Selection for the BOI Credit Officer Bharti 2026 will be through an Online Examination and/or Personal Interview, depending on the number of applicants. If both stages are conducted, the weightage will be 70:30 between the Online Examination and Interview. Scrutiny of Eligibility: Shortlisting based on documents uploaded at registration. Online Examination: 125 marks total - English Language (25 marks, qualifying nature) and Professional Knowledge (100 marks), composite duration of 120 minutes. Personal Interview: 100 marks, conducted for candidates shortlisted based on merit. Final Merit List: Prepared based on combined scores of Online Examination and Interview. The minimum qualifying marks in each test is 35% for General/EWS candidates, with a 5% relaxation for SC/ST/OBC/PWD candidates. There is a penalty of one-fourth marks for each wrong answer in the objective test.

content_sections.heading: Bank of India Credit Officer Recruitment 2026 Apply Online
content_sections.text: Candidates eligible for the Bank of India Credit Officer Apply Online 2026 process must register through the official website between 30 June 2026 and 20 July 2026. Below are the step-by-step instructions to complete the application. Visit www.bankofindia.bank.in and click on the "Career" section. Click on "Recruitment of Credit Officers in GBO Stream Project No. 2026-27/01." Click "Apply Online" and complete New Registration with Name, Contact details, and Email ID. Note the Provisional Registration Number and Password generated. Fill in personal, educational, and experience details, and upload photograph, signature, left thumb impression, and handwritten declaration as per specifications. Preview the application form and click "Complete Registration." Proceed to pay the application fee online. Take a printout of the system-generated application form and e-receipt for future reference. Direct Link to Apply Bank of India Credit Officer Recruitment 2026

content_sections.heading: Bank of India Credit Officer Recruitment 2026 Document Requirements
content_sections.text: Selected candidates called for interview must produce original documents along with self-attested photocopies, including proof of date of birth, educational mark sheets/certificates, category certificates (if applicable), and experience certificates. Candidates serving in Government/PSU offices must also submit a No Objection Certificate (NOC) from their employer. Selected candidates will also need to pay a refundable security deposit: ₹2,00,000 for MMGS-II and ₹3,00,000 for MMGS-III and SMGS-IV , refundable after 3 years of satisfactory service.

content_sections.heading: Common Mistakes to Avoid in Bank of India Credit Officer Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Many candidates make avoidable errors while applying for the Bank of India Credit Officer Bharti 2026 that can lead to rejection of their application. Uploading a photograph or signature that does not meet the prescribed dimensions or file size. Writing the handwritten declaration in capital letters or in a language other than English, making the application invalid. Selecting the wrong post or category without verifying eligibility from Annexure-I before submission. Submitting multiple applications, which leads to forfeiture of fees from earlier registrations. Failing to keep the registered email ID and mobile number active throughout the recruitment process.

faq.question: Q1. Has Bank of India officially released the Credit Officer Recruitment 2026 notification?
faq.answer: Yes, Bank of India released the official notification for Credit Officer (GBO Stream) Recruitment 2026 on 01 June 2026 under Project No. 2026-27/01.

faq.question: Q2. What is the last date to apply for Bank of India Credit Officer Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The last date to submit the online application is 20 July 2026.

faq.question: Q3. How many vacancies are available in Bank of India Credit Officer Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: There are 779 total vacancies across MMGS-II (677), MMGS-III (61), and SMGS-IV (41) grades.

faq.question: Q4. What is the application fee for Bank of India Credit Officer Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The fee is ₹175 for SC/ST/PWD candidates and ₹850 for General and other category candidates.

faq.question: Q5. What is the age limit for Bank of India Credit Officer posts?
faq.answer: Age limits vary by grade: 25-32 years for MMGS-II, 28-38 years for MMGS-III, and 32-45 years for SMGS-IV, as on 01 June 2026.

faq.question: Q6. What is the selection process for Bank of India Credit Officer Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Selection is through an Online Examination and/or Personal Interview, with a 70:30 weightage if both stages are conducted.

faq.question: Q7. What is the salary for Bank of India Credit Officer posts?
faq.answer: Pay scales range from ₹64,820 for MMGS-II up to ₹1,20,940 for SMGS-IV, along with other allowances.

faq.question: Q8. What is the minimum qualification required for Bank of India Credit Officer Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates need a graduation degree with 60% marks (55% for SC/ST/OBC/PWBD) or CA/CMA-ICWA/CS/CFA, along with relevant credit/banking experience.

faq.question: Q9. How can candidates apply for Bank of India Credit Officer Recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates can apply online through www.bankofindia.bank.in under the Career section from 30 June 2026 to 20 July 2026.

faq.question: Q10. What is the security deposit required for selected candidates?
faq.answer: Selected candidates must deposit ₹2,00,000 (MMGS-II) or ₹3,00,000 (MMGS-III/SMGS-IV), refundable after 3 years of satisfactory service.
